results.rendered_html: ut-kala m. (perhaps fr. ud-√kal?) N. of the country Orissa (see Oḍra; the word is interpreted to mean ‘the glorious country’ [ Hunter ]; or ‘lying beyond, the outlying strip of land’ [ Beames ]; according to others it merely means ‘the country of bird-catchers’)

results.rendered_html:
utkala m.
1〉 pl. N. eines Volkes, Bewohner von Orissa (oḍra),  TRIK. 2,1,11 .  MBH. 7,122 .  R. 4,41,14 . 40,25 , v. l.  VARĀH. BṚH. S. _in_ Verz. d. B. H. No. 849 (14) .  RAGH. 4,38 .  VP. 186 .  COLEBR. Misc. Ess. II, 179 . der Ursprung des Volkes wird auf utkala, einen Sohn Sudyumnaʼs, zurückgeführt  HARIV. 631. fg.   VP. 350 .
— 2〉 Vogelsteller.
— 3〉 Lastträger (m.f.n.)  ŚABDAM. _im_ ŚKDR.

results.rendered_html: utkala or oḍ N. of a country, the modern Orissa, which lay to the south of Tāmralipta, and extended to the river Kapiśā; cf.  R. 4. 38.  The chief to was of this province are Cuttak and Purī where the celebrated temple of Jagannātha is situated.

results.rendered_html:
utkala a. Excessive, piteous;  K. 306. 
— laḥ
1 N. of a country, the modern Orissa, or the inhabitants of that country (pl.); jagannāthaprāṃtadeśa utkalaḥ parikīrtitaḥ; see āṃḍra; utkalādarśitapathaḥ  R. 4. 38. 
2 A fowler, bird-catcher.
3 A porter (carrying a load with him).
4 A sub-division of Brāhmaṇas.
